query: Omni-Logic Plus OR Omni-Logic Immune OR Caricol OR Caricol Gastro OR Gut Health Reset Program OR Dynamic Probiotic Duo
answer: Omni‑Logic Plus is a vegetarian prebiotic powder that blends galacto‑oligosaccharide (GOS), fructo‑oligosaccharide (FOS), konjac root extract, calcium carbonate, gum arabic, zinc citrate, cholecalciferol (vitamin D3) and riboflavin, and it contains about 1.14 g of lactose per daily dose while remaining gluten‑, soy‑, yeast‑ and GMO‑free; the recommended use is to stir one measuring spoon (≈5 g) into 6 oz of water or juice, ideally three times a day after a gradual start, and it is safe for pregnant or breastfeeding women and for those trying to conceive. Omni‑Logic Immune is another prebiotic that focuses on acacia fiber (gum arabic) and resistant starch, also free of gluten, soy, corn, nuts and GMOs, with the same dosing guidance and safety profile (including suitability for children over six on a reduced dose). Caricol and Caricol Gastro are Omni‑Biotic probiotic blends formulated to support digestive health and gastrointestinal comfort, offering clinically studied strains for regular bowel function and overall gut balance. The Gut Health Reset Program is a three‑month regimen that sequentially delivers Omni‑Biotic AB 10 (to reduce pathogenic bacteria, viruses and yeasts), followed by Omni‑Biotic Stress Release and finally Omni‑Biotic Balance, each mixed into a non‑acidic liquid on an empty stomach to “reset” the microbiome and promote lasting wellness. The Dynamic Probiotic Duo pairs Omni‑Biotic Balance for morning use with Omni‑Biotic Stress Release for evening use, delivering a comprehensive gut‑brain support system that enhances digestion, immune function, stress resilience, cognition and mood, and like the other Omni‑Biotic offerings it is vegan, lactose‑, gluten‑, soy‑free and suitable for active lifestyles.

## FAQ

#### What is the Dynamic Probiotic Duo?

This bundle is designed to support your physical and mental health. Based on over 30 years of gut microbiome research, the Dynamic Probiotic Duo is curated by Omni-Biotic’s Founder, Anita Frauwallner, with the goal to help you achieve your most vital self.

In the morning, you take Omni-Biotic Balance to optimize the gut flora, support healthy digestion, and balance immune function.

In the evening, you take Omni-Biotic Stress Release probiotic to replenish the good bacteria in the gut, repair the gut barrier, reduce local and systemic inflammation, and improve cognition and mood.

#### What are the Health Benefits of this Bundle?

Taking these complimentary Omni-Biotic probiotics will:

#### Who is the Dynamic Probiotic Duo Best Suited For?

The Dynamic Probiotic Duo is best suited for individuals who are seeking to support both their physical and mental wellbeing by restoring and nourishing their gut health. It's a comprehensive solution that supports both body and mind, making it ideal for people with active lifestyles or those who face regular stressors but want to remain physically and mentally sharp.

#### What Strains of Beneficial Bacteria Are Included in this Bundle?

Omni-Biotic Stress Release contains nine specially selected bacterial strains:

Lactobacillus casei W56  
Lactococcus lactis W19  
Lactobacillus acidophilus W22  
Bifidobacterium lactis W52  
Lactobacillus paracasei W20  
Lactobacillus plantarum W62  
Bifidobacterium lactis W51  
Bifidobacterium bifidum W23  
Lactobacillus salivarius W24

Omni-Biotic Balance contains six specially selected bacterial strains:

Bifidobacterium animals W53  
Lactobacillus acidophilus W55  
Lactobacillus salivarius W57  
Enterococcus faecium W54  
Lactococcus lactis W58  
Lactobacillus casei W56

#### Why Are There Other Ingredients Included in Omni-Biotic Probiotics?

The freeze-dried powder of Omni-Biotic probiotics contains different prebiotic ingredients that serve as food for the beneficial bacteria. When the probiotic powder is dissolved in water prior to intake, the freeze-dried probiotic bacteria rehydrate and begin to consume these prebiotic nutrients. This makes them strong for their passage through the digestive tract, and ensures high probiotic survival rates into the gut.

If you extend the activation time of Omni-Biotic to up to 30 minutes, the bacteria almost completely metabolize their food so that, at most, only a few molecules of these substances enter your body.

#### Which Probiotic Formulation Should I Continue With After Completing This Bundle?

You can stick with your favorite probiotic formulation from the Dynamic Probiotic Duo or you can take our [Probiotic Fit Quiz](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/take-quiz/) to find the best formulation for your current health needs. We’re also happy to answer any questions and help guide you – contact us at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914.

#### How to Best Take the Probiotics in the Dynamic Probiotic Duo?

For this bundle it is recommended to take Omni-Biotic Balance in the morning and Omni-Biotic Stress Release in the evening. Please note that Omni-Biotic probiotics are best taken on an empty stomach. 

#### Can I Take the Dynamic Probiotic Duo Alongside Other Medications/Supplements?

Yes, Omni-Biotic probiotics contain only strains of bacteria that naturally occur in the human intestine, and no chemical agents that can interact with other supplements or medications.

It is, however, recommended to take Omni-Biotic probiotics at least one hour away from other supplements and/or medications.

#### Can I Dissolve Omni-Biotic in Anything Other than Water?

Omni-Biotic probiotics are manufactured in the form of freeze-dried bacteria that need to be reactivated before intake. Due to its neutral pH value, room temperature water creates the optimal environment to activate the bacteria and to energize them for their journey through your gastro-intestinal tract.

If you prefer, milk or yogurt, as well as apple juice are also well suited to rehydrate Omni-Biotic.

Please note that, in general, the liquid in which you dissolve Omni-Biotic should be under 100 degrees Fahrenheit and non-acidic. If the liquid is too cold, the bacteria cannot sufficiently “wake up” from their freeze-dried state. If the liquid is too hot, then they will die before you drink them. Juices and coffee are too acidic for the probiotic bacteria to survive.

#### Are There Any Side Effects of Using this Product?

Depending on the health of your gut microbiome, it may be that you experience mild digestive issues (e.g. mild bloating, gas, diarrhea) the first few days of using this product. This is a sign that your gut flora is rebalancing and potentially eliminating any pathogens. If this is the case, it is recommended that you introduce Omni-Biotic slowly over the course of a few days (beginning with the amount of a knife-tip) to minimize possible digestive side effects. Such situations are rare and only occur in about 5% of users.

#### How Should the Product be Stored?

All Omni-Biotic probiotics are shelf-stable at room temperature. They should be stored in a cool and dry place, and do not need to be refrigerated.

#### What is the Shelf Life of this Product?

Omni-Biotic probiotics have a shelf life of 12-18 months.

#### Do Omni-Biotic Probiotics Have Regulatory Approval?

All Omni-Biotic products available in the U.S. have FDA GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) status and meet the quality criteria for medically relevant pro and synbiotics.

Omni-Biotic products are developed by Institut AllergoSan in accordance with quality criteria of the World Health Organization (WHO), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the World Gastroenterology Organization (WGO), and the Austrian/German Societies for Probiotic Medicine (OePROM / DePROM).

#### Are Omni-Biotic Probiotics Suitable For Vegetarians And Vegans?

Almost all Omni-Biotic products available on omnibioticlife.com are certified vegan, as well as lactose, gluten, and soy-free, and made up of non-GMO ingredients. Please note that our Omni-Logic Plus prebiotic contains a small amount of lactose and is therefore considered vegetarian.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Pregnant or Breastfeeding Women?

Yes, all Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for use during pregnancy, post-partum, while breastfeeding and when trying to conceive. In fact, taking a high-quality probiotic supplement during pregnancy and the first few months after birth will help support a healthy pregnancy and help lay the foundation for the baby’s health.

That being said, it is always best to check with your individual healthcare practitioner for all supplements you are taking, especially during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Children?

Yes, most Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for children. Omni-Biotic Hetox is not recommended for children.

For babies and up to one year of age: Omni-Biotic Panda was specifically developed for babies and young children to support the healthy development of the baby’s gut microbiome, digestive system, and immune function. It has also been shown in clinical studies to improve symptoms of infant colic and to reduce the risk of childhood allergies, such as eczema.

For children between one and three years of age: If the child is benefiting from Omni-Biotic Panda, this is often a good choice to continue until three years of age. Other probiotic formulations (except Omni-Biotic Hetox) can be taken at half of one sachet per day (i.e., half of the daily recommended dose for adults).

For children older than three years of age: Children above three years of age typically respond well to taking the regular daily recommended dose.

To improve the taste of the probiotic, you can dissolve it in infant formula, milk or yogurt, a dairy alternative, lukewarm soups and apple juice. Avoid juices that are too acidic such as orange juice as these negatively impact the probiotic bacteria.

#### What is Your Return Policy?

To initiate a return or exchange, please kindly contact our Customer Service team at info@omnibioticlife.com or (914) 615-9914. Claims for shortages or damages must be reported within 10 days of receiving your order.

## FAQ

#### What is the Dynamic Probiotic Duo?

This bundle is designed to support your physical and mental health. Based on over 30 years of gut microbiome research, the Dynamic Probiotic Duo is curated by Omni-Biotic’s Founder, Anita Frauwallner, with the goal to help you achieve your most vital self.

In the morning, you take Omni-Biotic Balance to optimize the gut flora, support healthy digestion, and balance immune function.

In the evening, you take Omni-Biotic Stress Release probiotic to replenish the good bacteria in the gut, repair the gut barrier, reduce local and systemic inflammation, and improve cognition and mood.

#### What are the Health Benefits of this Bundle?

Taking these complimentary Omni-Biotic probiotics will:

#### Who is the Dynamic Probiotic Duo Best Suited For?

The Dynamic Probiotic Duo is best suited for individuals who are seeking to support both their physical and mental wellbeing by restoring and nourishing their gut health. It's a comprehensive solution that supports both body and mind, making it ideal for people with active lifestyles or those who face regular stressors but want to remain physically and mentally sharp.

#### What Strains of Beneficial Bacteria Are Included in this Bundle?

Omni-Biotic Stress Release contains nine specially selected bacterial strains:

Lactobacillus casei W56  
Lactococcus lactis W19  
Lactobacillus acidophilus W22  
Bifidobacterium lactis W52  
Lactobacillus paracasei W20  
Lactobacillus plantarum W62

Omni-Biotic Balance contains six specially selected bacterial strains:

Bifidobacterium animals W53  
Lactobacillus acidophilus W55  
Lactobacillus salivarius W57  
Enterococcus faecium W54  
Lactococcus lactis W58  
Lactobacillus casei W56

#### Why Are There Other Ingredients Included in Omni-Biotic Probiotics?

The freeze-dried powder of Omni-Biotic probiotics contains different prebiotic ingredients that serve as food for the beneficial bacteria. When the probiotic powder is dissolved in water prior to intake, the freeze-dried probiotic bacteria rehydrate and begin to consume these prebiotic nutrients. This makes them strong for their passage through the digestive tract, and ensures high probiotic survival rates into the gut.

If you extend the activation time of Omni-Biotic to up to 30 minutes, the bacteria almost completely metabolize their food so that, at most, only a few molecules of these substances enter your body.

#### Which Probiotic Formulation Should I Continue With After Completing This Bundle?

You can stick with your favorite probiotic formulation from the Dynamic Probiotic Duo or you can take our [Probiotic Fit Quiz](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/take-quiz/) to find the best formulation for your current health needs. We’re also happy to answer any questions and help guide you – contact us at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914.

#### How to Best Take the Probiotics in the Dynamic Probiotic Duo?

For this bundle it is recommended to take Omni-Biotic Balance in the morning and Omni-Biotic Stress Release in the evening. Please note that Omni-Biotic probiotics are best taken on an empty stomach. 

#### Can I Take the Dynamic Probiotic Duo Alongside Other Medications/Supplements?

Yes, Omni-Biotic probiotics contain only strains of bacteria that naturally occur in the human intestine, and no chemical agents that can interact with other supplements or medications.

It is, however, recommended to take Omni-Biotic probiotics at least one hour away from other supplements and/or medications.

#### Can I Dissolve Omni-Biotic in Anything Other than Water?

Omni-Biotic probiotics are manufactured in the form of freeze-dried bacteria that need to be reactivated before intake. Due to its neutral pH value, room temperature water creates the optimal environment to activate the bacteria and to energize them for their journey through your gastro-intestinal tract.

If you prefer, milk or yogurt, as well as apple juice are also well suited to rehydrate Omni-Biotic.

Please note that, in general, the liquid in which you dissolve Omni-Biotic should be under 100 degrees Fahrenheit and non-acidic. If the liquid is too cold, the bacteria cannot sufficiently “wake up” from their freeze-dried state. If the liquid is too hot, then they will die before you drink them. Juices and coffee are too acidic for the probiotic bacteria to survive.

#### Are There Any Side Effects of Using this Product?

Depending on the health of your gut microbiome, it may be that you experience mild digestive issues (e.g. mild bloating, gas, diarrhea) the first few days of using this product. This is a sign that your gut flora is rebalancing and potentially eliminating any pathogens. If this is the case, it is recommended that you introduce Omni-Biotic slowly over the course of a few days (beginning with the amount of a knife-tip) to minimize possible digestive side effects. Such situations are rare and only occur in about 5% of users.

#### How Should the Product be Stored?

All Omni-Biotic probiotics are shelf-stable at room temperature. They should be stored in a cool and dry place, and do not need to be refrigerated.

#### What is the Shelf Life of this Product?

Omni-Biotic probiotics have a shelf life of 12-18 months.

#### Do Omni-Biotic Probiotics Have Regulatory Approval?

All Omni-Biotic products available in the U.S. have FDA GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) status and meet the quality criteria for medically relevant pro and synbiotics.

Omni-Biotic products are developed by Institut AllergoSan in accordance with quality criteria of the World Health Organization (WHO), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the World Gastroenterology Organization (WGO), and the Austrian/German Societies for Probiotic Medicine (OePROM / DePROM).

#### Are Omni-Biotic Probiotics Suitable For Vegetarians And Vegans?

Almost all Omni-Biotic products available on omnibioticlife.com are certified vegan, as well as lactose, gluten, and soy-free, and made up of non-GMO ingredients. Please note that our Omni-Logic Plus prebiotic contains a small amount of lactose and is therefore considered vegetarian.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Pregnant or Breastfeeding Women?

Yes, all Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for use during pregnancy, post-partum, while breastfeeding and when trying to conceive. In fact, taking a high-quality probiotic supplement during pregnancy and the first few months after birth will help support a healthy pregnancy and help lay the foundation for the baby’s health.

That being said, it is always best to check with your individual healthcare practitioner for all supplements you are taking, especially during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Children?

Yes, most Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for children. Omni-Biotic Hetox is not recommended for children.

For babies and up to one year of age: Omni-Biotic Panda was specifically developed for babies and young children to support the healthy development of the baby’s gut microbiome, digestive system, and immune function. It has also been shown in clinical studies to improve symptoms of infant colic and to reduce the risk of childhood allergies, such as eczema.

For children between one and three years of age: If the child is benefiting from Omni-Biotic Panda, this is often a good choice to continue until three years of age. Other probiotic formulations (except Omni-Biotic Hetox) can be taken at half of one sachet per day (i.e., half of the daily recommended dose for adults).

For children older than three years of age: Children above three years of age typically respond well to taking the regular daily recommended dose.

To improve the taste of the probiotic, you can dissolve it in infant formula, milk or yogurt, a dairy alternative, lukewarm soups and apple juice. Avoid juices that are too acidic such as orange juice as these negatively impact the probiotic bacteria.

#### What is Your Return Policy?

To initiate a return or exchange, please kindly contact our Customer Service team at info@omnibioticlife.com or (914) 615-9914. Claims for shortages or damages must be reported within 10 days of receiving your order.

## FAQ

#### What is the Gut Health Reset Program?

This three-month probiotic program is designed to reset the gut microbiome and support overall wellness. Based on over 30 years of gut microbiome research, this program is curated by Omni-Biotic’s Founder, Anita Frauwallner, with the goal of helping you achieve your most vital self.

For month 1, you take the Omni-Biotic AB 10 probiotic to eliminate bad bacteria, viruses, and yeasts. These build up over time and contribute to digestive issues.

In month 2, you take the Omni-Biotic Stress Release probiotic to replenish the good bacteria in the gut, repair the gut barrier, and reduce local and systemic inflammation.

In month 3, you take the Omni-Biotic Balance probiotic to rebalance the gut flora, support healthy digestion, immune function, cognition, and detoxification.

#### What are the Health Benefits of this Program?

Three months of taking these specific Omni-Biotic probiotics will:

* Restore bacterial diversity in the gut microbiome
* Improve gut barrier function
* Reduce inflammation
* Optimize digestive comfort and function
* Support healthy immune response
* Strengthen the gut-brain axis

#### Who is this Program Best Suited For?

Our Gut Health Reset Program is recommended for those who are starting out on their gut healing journey as well as people who are looking to restore their gut health following infection, use of antimicrobial medication, and years of high stress living.

#### What Strains of Beneficial Bacteria Are Included in this Program?

Omni-Biotic AB 10 contains ten specially selected, scientifically tested human bacterial strains.

#### Why Are There Other Ingredients Included in Each Omni-Biotic Probiotic?

The freeze-dried powder of Omni-Biotic probiotics contains different prebiotic ingredients that serve as food for the beneficial bacteria. When the probiotic powder is dissolved in water prior to intake, the freeze-dried probiotic bacteria rehydrate and begin to consume these prebiotic nutrients. This makes them strong for their passage through the digestive tract, and ensures high probiotic survival rates into the gut.

If you extend the activation time of Omni-Biotic to up to 30 minutes, the bacteria almost completely metabolize their food so that, at most, only a few molecules of these substances enter your body.

#### Which Probiotic Formulation Should I Continue With After Completing This Program?

You can stick with your favorite probiotic formulation from the Gut Health Reset Program. Or, you can take our [Probiotic Fit Quiz](/pages/probiotic-fit-quiz "Probiotic Fit Quiz") to find the best formulation for your current health needs. We’re also happy to answer any questions and help guide you – contact us at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914.

#### How to Best Take the Probiotics in the Gut Health Reset Program?

During the Gut Health Reset Program, you will seamlessly transition through a curated sequence of probiotics over a three-month period. You begin the program with Omni-Biotic AB 10, followed by Omni-Biotic Stress Release, and lastly Omni-Biotic Balance. 

1. Stir one sachet of Omni-Biotic probiotics in approximately 4 ounces of room temperature water or other non-acidic liquid (i.e. dairy/dairy alternative milk or yogurt, apple juice).
2. Wait 1 minute for probiotic activation.
3. Stir again and enjoy!

Please note that Omni-Biotic probiotics are best taken on an empty stomach.

#### Can I Take the Gut Health Reset Program Alongside Other Medications/Supplements?

Yes, Omni-Biotic probiotics contain only strains of bacteria that naturally occur in the human intestine, and no chemical agents that can interact with other supplements or medications.

It is, however, recommended to take Omni-Biotic probiotics at least one hour away from other supplements and/or medications.

#### Can I Dissolve Omni-Biotic in Anything Other than Water?

Omni-Biotic probiotics are manufactured in the form of freeze-dried bacteria that need to be rehydrated before intake. Due to its neutral pH value, room temperature water creates the optimal environment to activate the bacteria and to energize them for their journey through your gastro-intestinal tract.

If you prefer, milk or yogurt, as well as apple juice are also well suited to rehydrate Omni-Biotic.

Please note that, in general, the liquid in which you dissolve Omni-Biotic should be under 100 degrees Fahrenheit and non-acidic. If the liquid is too cold, the bacteria cannot sufficiently “wake up” from their freeze-dried state. If the liquid is too hot, then they will die before you drink them. Juices and coffee are too acidic for the probiotic bacteria to survive. It is also not recommended to throw the Omni-Biotic powder into a power blender as part of a smoothie mix.

#### Are There Any Side Effects of Using this Product?

Depending on the health of your gut microbiome, it may be that you experience mild digestive issues (e.g. mild bloating, gas, diarrhea) the first few days of using this product. This is a sign that your gut flora is rebalancing and potentially eliminating any pathogens.

If this is the case, it is recommended that you introduce Omni-Biotic slowly over the course of a few days (beginning with the amount of a knife-tip) to minimize possible digestive issues. Such situations are rare and only occur in about 5% of users.

#### How Should the Product be Stored?

All Omni-Biotic probiotics are shelf-stable at room temperature. They should be stored in a cool and dry place, but do not need to be refrigerated.

#### What is the Shelf Life of this Product?

Omni-Biotic probiotics have a shelf life of 12-18 months.

#### Do Omni-Biotic Probiotics Have Regulatory Approval?

All Omni-Biotic products available in the U.S. have FDA GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) status and meet the quality criteria for medically relevant pro and synbiotics.

Omni-Biotic products are developed by Institut AllergoSan in accordance with quality criteria of the World Health Organization (WHO), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the World Gastroenterology Organization (WGO), and the Austrian/German Societies for Probiotic Medicine (OePROM / DePROM).

#### Are Omni-Biotic Probiotics Suitable For Vegetarians and Vegans?

Almost all Omni-Biotic products available on omnibioticlife.com are certified vegan, as well as lactose, gluten, and soy-free, and made up of non-GMO ingredients.

Please note that our Omni-Logic Plus prebiotic contains a small amount of lactose, and is therefore considered to be vegetarian.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Pregnant or Breastfeeding Women?

Yes, all Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for use during pregnancy, post-partum, while breastfeeding and when trying to conceive. In fact, taking a high-quality probiotic supplement during pregnancy and the first few months after birth will help support a healthy pregnancy and lay the foundation for baby’s health.

That being said, it is always best to check with your healthcare practitioner for all supplements you are taking, especially during pregnancy and while breastfeeding.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Children?

Yes, most Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for children. Omni-Biotic Hetox is not recommended for children.

For babies and up to one year of age: Omni-Biotic Panda was specifically developed for babies and young children to support the healthy development of the baby’s gut microbiome, digestive system, and immune function. It has also been shown in clinical studies to improve symptoms of infant colic and to reduce the risk of childhood allergies, such as eczema.

For children between one and three years of age: If the child is benefiting from Omni-Biotic Panda, this is often a good choice to continue until three years of age. Other probiotic formulations (except Omni-Biotic Hetox) can be taken at half of one sachet per day (i.e., half of the daily recommended dose for adults).

For children older than three years of age: Children above three years of age typically respond well to taking the regular daily recommended dose.

To improve the taste of the probiotic, you can dissolve it in baby formula, milk or yogurt, non-dairy milk or yogurt, lukewarm soups and apple juice. Avoid juices that are too acidic, such as orange juice, as these negatively impact the probiotic bacteria.

#### What is Your Return Policy?

To initiate a return or exchange, please kindly contact our Customer Service team at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914. Claims for shortages or damages must be reported within 10 days of receiving your order.

## FAQ

#### What is the Gut Health Reset Program?

This three-month probiotic program is designed to reset the gut microbiome and support overall wellness. Based on over 30 years of gut microbiome research, this program is curated by Omni-Biotic’s Founder, Anita Frauwallner, with the goal of helping you achieve your most vital self.

For month 1, you take the Omni-Biotic AB 10 probiotic to eliminate bad bacteria, viruses, and yeasts. These build up over time and contribute to digestive issues.

In month 2, you take the Omni-Biotic Stress Release probiotic to replenish the good bacteria in the gut, repair the gut barrier, and reduce local and systemic inflammation.

In month 3, you take the Omni-Biotic Balance probiotic to rebalance the gut flora, support healthy digestion, immune function, cognition, and detoxification.

#### What are the Health Benefits of this Program?

Three months of taking these specific Omni-Biotic probiotics will:

* Restore bacterial diversity in the gut microbiome
* Improve gut barrier function
* Reduce inflammation
* Optimize digestive comfort and function
* Support healthy immune response
* Strengthen the gut-brain axis

#### Who is this Program Best Suited For?

Our Gut Health Reset Program is recommended for those who are starting out on their gut healing journey as well as people who are looking to restore their gut health following infection, use of antimicrobial medication, and years of high stress living.

#### What Strains of Beneficial Bacteria Are Included in this Program?

Omni-Biotic AB 10 contains ten specially selected, scientifically tested human bacterial strains.

Omni-Biotic Stress Release contains nine specially selected, scientifically tested human bacterial strains.

Omni-Biotic Balance contains six important, highly active, human bacterial strains that inhabit every inch of the intestine.

#### Why Are There Other Ingredients Included in Each Omni-Biotic Probiotic?

The freeze-dried powder of Omni-Biotic probiotics contains different prebiotic ingredients that serve as food for the beneficial bacteria. When the probiotic powder is dissolved in water prior to intake, the freeze-dried probiotic bacteria rehydrate and begin to consume these prebiotic nutrients. This makes them strong for their passage through the digestive tract, and ensures high probiotic survival rates into the gut.

If you extend the activation time of Omni-Biotic to up to 30 minutes, the bacteria almost completely metabolize their food so that, at most, only a few molecules of these substances enter your body.

#### Which Probiotic Formulation Should I Continue With After Completing This Program?

You can stick with your favorite probiotic formulation from the Gut Health Reset Program. Or, you can take our [Probiotic Fit Quiz](/pages/probiotic-fit-quiz "Probiotic Fit Quiz") to find the best formulation for your current health needs. We’re also happy to answer any questions and help guide you – contact us at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914.

#### How to Best Take the Probiotics in the Gut Health Reset Program?

During the Gut Health Reset Program, you will seamlessly transition through a curated sequence of probiotics over a three-month period. You begin the program with Omni-Biotic AB 10, followed by Omni-Biotic Stress Release, and lastly Omni-Biotic Balance. 

1. Stir one sachet of Omni-Biotic probiotics in approximately 4 ounces of room temperature water or other non-acidic liquid (i.e. dairy/dairy alternative milk or yogurt, apple juice).
2. Wait 1 minute for probiotic activation.
3. Stir again and enjoy!

Please note that Omni-Biotic probiotics are best taken on an empty stomach.

#### Can I Take the Gut Health Reset Program Alongside Other Medications/Supplements?

Yes, Omni-Biotic probiotics contain only strains of bacteria that naturally occur in the human intestine, and no chemical agents that can interact with other supplements or medications.

It is, however, recommended to take Omni-Biotic probiotics at least one hour away from other supplements and/or medications.

#### Can I Dissolve Omni-Biotic in Anything Other than Water?

Omni-Biotic probiotics are manufactured in the form of freeze-dried bacteria that need to be rehydrated before intake. Due to its neutral pH value, room temperature water creates the optimal environment to activate the bacteria and to energize them for their journey through your gastro-intestinal tract.

If you prefer, milk or yogurt, as well as apple juice are also well suited to rehydrate Omni-Biotic.

Please note that, in general, the liquid in which you dissolve Omni-Biotic should be under 100 degrees Fahrenheit and non-acidic. If the liquid is too cold, the bacteria cannot sufficiently “wake up” from their freeze-dried state. If the liquid is too hot, then they will die before you drink them. Juices and coffee are too acidic for the probiotic bacteria to survive. It is also not recommended to throw the Omni-Biotic powder into a power blender as part of a smoothie mix.

#### Are There Any Side Effects of Using this Product?

Depending on the health of your gut microbiome, it may be that you experience mild digestive issues (e.g. mild bloating, gas, diarrhea) the first few days of using this product. This is a sign that your gut flora is rebalancing and potentially eliminating any pathogens.

If this is the case, it is recommended that you introduce Omni-Biotic slowly over the course of a few days (beginning with the amount of a knife-tip) to minimize possible digestive issues. Such situations are rare and only occur in about 5% of users.

#### How Should the Product be Stored?

All Omni-Biotic probiotics are shelf-stable at room temperature. They should be stored in a cool and dry place, but do not need to be refrigerated.

#### What is the Shelf Life of this Product?

Omni-Biotic probiotics have a shelf life of 12-18 months.

#### Do Omni-Biotic Probiotics Have Regulatory Approval?

All Omni-Biotic products available in the U.S. have FDA GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) status and meet the quality criteria for medically relevant pro and synbiotics.

Omni-Biotic products are developed by Institut AllergoSan in accordance with quality criteria of the World Health Organization (WHO), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the World Gastroenterology Organization (WGO), and the Austrian/German Societies for Probiotic Medicine (OePROM / DePROM).

#### Are Omni-Biotic Probiotics Suitable For Vegetarians and Vegans?

Almost all Omni-Biotic products available on omnibioticlife.com are certified vegan, as well as lactose, gluten, and soy-free, and made up of non-GMO ingredients.

Please note that our Omni-Logic Plus prebiotic contains a small amount of lactose, and is therefore considered to be vegetarian.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Pregnant or Breastfeeding Women?

Yes, all Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for use during pregnancy, post-partum, while breastfeeding and when trying to conceive. In fact, taking a high-quality probiotic supplement during pregnancy and the first few months after birth will help support a healthy pregnancy and lay the foundation for baby’s health.

That being said, it is always best to check with your healthcare practitioner for all supplements you are taking, especially during pregnancy and while breastfeeding.

#### Is this Product Safe to Use for Children?

Yes, most Omni-Biotic probiotics are safe for children. Omni-Biotic Hetox is not recommended for children.

For babies and up to one year of age: Omni-Biotic Panda was specifically developed for babies and young children to support the healthy development of the baby’s gut microbiome, digestive system, and immune function. It has also been shown in clinical studies to improve symptoms of infant colic and to reduce the risk of childhood allergies, such as eczema.

For children between one and three years of age: If the child is benefiting from Omni-Biotic Panda, this is often a good choice to continue until three years of age. Other probiotic formulations (except Omni-Biotic Hetox) can be taken at half of one sachet per day (i.e., half of the daily recommended dose for adults).

For children older than three years of age: Children above three years of age typically respond well to taking the regular daily recommended dose.

To improve the taste of the probiotic, you can dissolve it in baby formula, milk or yogurt, non-dairy milk or yogurt, lukewarm soups and apple juice. Avoid juices that are too acidic, such as orange juice, as these negatively impact the probiotic bacteria.

#### What is Your Return Policy?

To initiate a return or exchange, please kindly contact our Customer Service team at info@omnibioticlife.com or 914-615-9914. Claims for shortages or damages must be reported within 10 days of receiving your order.

Precision Prebiotics

Prebiotics are insoluble dietary fibers that serve as the food for our good gut bacteria. Just like most living beings, different bacterial species thrive on different sources of dietary fiber. Mindfully formulated prebiotics are targeted dietary fiber supplements that help provide the necessary nutrients that our good gut bacteria need to thrive. This is also why consuming a diverse range of dietary fiber in your diet is important for gut health.  
  
Prebiotics are a particularly effective way to nurture and promote healthy amounts of the anaerobic bacteria living in the gut. These anaerobic bacteria cannot easily be made into probiotic supplements as they die when encountering oxygen. Prebiotic supplements provide the necessary fibers for these anaerobic bacteria to thrive, and they are incredibly important for our gut health and overall wellbeing.

## FAQ

#### What Are Prebiotics And The Health Benefits Of Taking A Prebiotic Supplement?

The probiotic bacteria that live in our gut microbiome (the world of billions of bacteria in our intestines) are split up into two groups: aerobic and anaerobic bacteria.

A large portion of these probiotic bacteria consist of anaerobic bacteria, which means that they do not live or grow when oxygen is present. Only a small percentage consists of the aerobic bacteria, which can survive in oxygen and can therefore be manufactured into dietary supplements. Due to their sensitivity to oxygen, the anaerobic bacteria cannot be taken in the form of probiotic supplements.

Luckily, scientists have come up with a clever way to promote the growth of anaerobic probiotic bacteria, which are incredibly important for our health. This is via prebiotics!

Prebiotics are specific dietary fibers that serve as the food for certain anaerobic probiotic bacteria. The gist is that, if you can’t supplement certain key, beneficial bacteria, you can make sure that you’re providing everything they need to thrive in your gut by taking a prebiotic supplement.

Just like people, every probiotic bacterium has a favorite food and eating this ensures optimal livelihood. For example, Bacteroidetes particularly like apple pectin, butyrate-forming faecalibacteria prefer fructo-oligosaccharides (FOS) and special milk sugars such as galacto-oligosaccharides (GOS), whereas Akkermansia muciniphila thrive on galacto-olgosaccharides (GOS).

#### Who Would Benefit From Taking A Prebiotic Supplement?

External factors, including taking medications such as antibiotics, high amounts of and prolonged periods of stress, as well as unhealthy eating habits can disrupt the healthy balance of our gut bacteria. This can give rise to gut issues such as gas, bloating, and changes to bowel movements, as well as to longer-term health challenges such as chronic inflammation, leaky gut, eczema and migraines.

If there is an unhealthy imbalance of bacteria in the gut, a prebiotic supplement can support the growth of anaerobic bacteria in order to restore healthy bacterial balance and diversity.

Taking a prebiotic supplement is also an effective way to add more fiber to your diet, which supports a healthy gut microbiome and helps to promote regular bowel movements.

## FAQ

#### What Are Prebiotics And The Health Benefits Of Taking A Prebiotic Supplement?

The probiotic bacteria that live in our gut microbiome (the world of billions of bacteria in our intestines) are split up into two groups: aerobic and anaerobic bacteria.

A large portion of these probiotic bacteria consist of anaerobic bacteria, which means that they do not live or grow when oxygen is present. Only a small percentage consists of the aerobic bacteria, which can survive in oxygen and can therefore be manufactured into dietary supplements. Due to their sensitivity to oxygen, the anaerobic bacteria cannot be taken in the form of probiotic supplements.

Luckily, scientists have come up with a clever way to promote the growth of anaerobic probiotic bacteria, which are incredibly important for our health. This is via prebiotics!

Prebiotics are specific dietary fibers that serve as the food for certain anaerobic probiotic bacteria. The gist is that, if you can’t supplement certain key, beneficial bacteria, you can make sure that you’re providing everything they need to thrive in your gut by taking a prebiotic supplement.

Just like people, every probiotic bacterium has a favorite food and eating this ensures optimal livelihood. For example, Bacteroidetes particularly like apple pectin, butyrate-forming faecalibacteria prefer fructo-oligosaccharides (FOS) and special milk sugars such as galacto-oligosaccharides (GOS), whereas Akkermansia muciniphila thrive on galacto-olgosaccharides (GOS).

# Fiber and Probiotics: Better Together?

Updated on    by   Sara Chatfield

Share

* [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://omnibioticlife.com)
* [Twitter](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://omnibioticlife.com&text=)
* [Pinterest](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=//omnibioticlife.com/cdn/shopifycloud/storefront/assets/no-image-2048-a2addb12_1100x1100.gif&url=https://omnibioticlife.com&description=)
* [Copy Link](https://omnibioticlife.com)

![Close-up of fresh blueberries, a fiber-rich food that supports gut health alongside probiotics](//omnibioticlife.com/cdn/shop/articles/blueberries-fiber-gut-health.webp?v=1769124601&width=3840)

Probiotics and fiber are both critical for digestion and can improve common digestive ailments. Dietary fiber comes from plant foods and promotes digestive regularity. Some specialized types of fiber, called prebiotics, provide a food source for gut bacteria and may enhance the effects of probiotics.  
  
[Probiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/what-are-probiotics/) are beneficial bacteria found in dietary supplements and some foods. Fiber and probiotics can work together to support a healthy, balanced gut microbiome, which is critical for digestive and overall health.  
  
If you suffer from common digestive issues like [constipation](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/), [gas and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/), or [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/), you may have heard the advice to increase your fiber intake. Consuming adequate dietary fiber can improve your digestion and help with common gastrointestinal ailments.  
  
You may have also heard of using probiotics, live good bacteria, to improve gut health. But what about taking probiotics and fiber together? Certain types of fiber can work in tandem with probiotics to benefit digestive health, and this combination can be a key element of a probiotic and fiber diet.

## How Fiber and Probiotics Support One Another

Eating more fiber has been linked to improved digestive and overall health. High-fiber diets can prevent or relieve constipation and also reduce the risk of several chronic diseases. Unfortunately, many of our diets are too low in fiber due to a lack of whole plant foods and an over-reliance on animal products and highly processed foods.  
  
Estimates indicate that [95% of Americans](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6124841/) don’t meet the recommended dietary fiber intake of 19-38 grams daily. Western-style diets, including the typical U.S. diet, are also associated with lower diversity of beneficial gut microbes compared to [diets higher in fiber from plant foods](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33693453/).  
  
Dietary fiber is typically categorized into soluble and insoluble forms. Both are important for digestive health. Soluble fiber can dissolve in water to form a gel and helps to soften the stool. It also slows nutrient absorption and increases satiety. Insoluble fiber provides bulk for easier passage of stool. Most foods provide a mix of fiber types.

Common types of fiber include:

* **Insoluble sources:** Cellulose, hemicellulose, and lignin, found mainly in vegetables and whole grains.
* **Soluble sources:** Pectin, gums, and mucilages; pectins are found primarily in fruits and vegetables, whereas gums and mucilages are mainly extracted from plants for use in [processed foods](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5390821/).

[Prebiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/prebiotics-guide/) are specific types of soluble fiber that pass undigested through the upper gastrointestinal tract and go on to provide nourishment for the beneficial bacteria in the colon. They can work together with probiotics, a direct source of live beneficial bacteria. Prebiotics act as the food source for our gut bacteria, whereas probiotic supplements deliver live, beneficial bacteria to the gut to support balanced and diverse gut flora.

## What are the Benefits of Combining Fiber with Probiotics?

![Close-up of fiber-rich fruits and vegetables including pears, green apple, artichoke, and cruciferous vegetables](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/fruits-vegtables-for-health.webp?v=1743544037)

Both probiotics and dietary fiber can be beneficial for digestive health. But what happens when you combine them?

### The Role of Probiotics

Probiotics offer many benefits for digestive health, including enhanced digestion, protection against bad bacteria, and a reduction in digestive symptoms like [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/) and [constipation](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/).  
  
Although some foods, such as yogurt, kefir, sauerkraut, kimchi, and kombucha, are cultured with or have added probiotics, not all [probiotic foods](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/kombucha-vs-yogurt-vs-omnibiotic/) have enough live bacteria to survive the digestive system to provide health benefits. They may also not have the right combination of strains to have a lasting impact on your gut health.  
  
On the other hand, probiotic supplements typically provide information on the [CFU (colony-forming units)](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/what-are-colony-forming-units/) per gram of live active microorganisms, which ideally will be 107 to 1011.  
  
Finding a product with multiple strains of beneficial bacteria may be preferable since much of the research on common digestive issues, such as antibiotic-associated diarrhea and symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, has used [multi-strain products](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/types-of-probiotics/).

### The Role of Fiber

Adequate fiber intake is critical for digestive health; it keeps your gastrointestinal tract moving and your bowel movements regular and helps prevent chronic conditions.  
   
Increasing your intake of whole plant foods high in fiber, including whole grains, fruits, vegetables, legumes, nuts, and seeds, will provide a variety of insoluble and soluble dietary fibers, along with other crucial nutrients and beneficial plant compounds.  
   
Fiber supplements can be helpful if you’re looking to add a specific fiber source. Some varieties of fiber offer more benefits than others, depending on your health goals.

![Probiotic powder being mixed with water to prepare a gut health supplement](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/probiotics-powder.webp?v=1743544037)

### Combining Probiotics and Fiber

If you’re supplementing both probiotics and additional fiber, it’s typically [best to take them separately](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/can-you-take-prebiotics-and-probiotics-together/) since some of the beneficial bacteria can adhere to the fiber and be eliminated rather than [absorbed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7817516/).  
   
Prebiotic fibers work with the good bacteria in the digestive system by increasing their numbers and activity, including their ability to support immunity and maintain the gut barrier [against harmful pathogens](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Prebiotics can also enhance the benefits of probiotics.  
   
It’s preferable not to take probiotics and prebiotic fiber simultaneously because if the probiotics ferment the prebiotics too soon, this can cause [bothersome symptoms](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177) like [abdominal pain and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/).  
   
Synbiotics designed to be combined are the exception.   
[Synbiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/can-you-take-prebiotics-and-probiotics-together/) are combination products that typically contain probiotics with a prebiotic, most commonly inulin, GOS, or FOS, that supports its beneficial [health effects](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7581511/). Research has found that synbiotics can enhance the microbiota, increasing beneficial *Bifidobacteria* and [*Lactobacilli*](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5622781/).  
   
The goal of a synbiotic is to maximize the health impact of both the good bacteria and the prebiotic fiber by providing them together. The amount of fiber in synbiotics varies, from very small quantities to larger amounts. Look for products that provide an optimal blend of probiotics with prebiotics.  
   
Omni-Biotics’ probiotic products are all synbiotics. Each Omni-Biotic probiotic contains prebiotic nutrients specifically selected for the probiotic strains in the formulation. These prebiotic fibers act as the food source for the specific probiotic bacteria in the Omni-Biotic powder. When Omni-Biotic probiotics are dissolved in water prior to intake, the probiotic bacteria rehydrate and consume these prebiotic fibers. This makes them strong for their passage through the GI tract, ensuring they arrive in the intestine alive and metabolically active.

## Is it Better to Take Prebiotics or Another Kind of Fiber?

Although all prebiotics are considered fiber, not all dietary fibers have prebiotic effects.   
The benefits of prebiotics for digestion go beyond those of standard fiber since they nourish beneficial bacteria, leading to a healthier, more balanced gut microbiome.  
   
Though humans have been consuming prebiotics in food since prehistoric times, research has uncovered their health benefits [much more recently](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Well-studied prebiotics include inulin, galactooligosaccharides (GOS), fructooligosaccharides (FOS), and resistant starch.  
   
[Prebiotics are found naturally](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/prebiotics-guide/) in some plant foods like chicory, artichokes, asparagus, onions, garlic, whole wheat, oats, and barley, as well as in some processed foods with added prebiotics. They are also available as supplements.   
Prebiotics target good bacteria in the colon, particularly *Bifidobacteria*. Research has found that consumption of the prebiotics [inulin, FOS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/), [resistant starch, and GOS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4949558/) significantly increased levels of *Bifidobacteria* and other beneficial gut bacteria.  
   
The benefits to gut flora are specific to fiber with prebiotic effects. A recent analysis of randomized controlled trials found that prebiotic fiber supplements lead to significantly higher counts of *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli* than [non-prebiotic fiber supplements](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366).  
   
In addition to enhancing their diversity and activity, prebiotics can increase the gut bacteria’s production of short-chain fatty acids. These acids are critical in supporting the gut barrier and lowering colon pH. In turn, this promotes the growth of beneficial bacteria and [inhibits gut pathogens](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Enhanced production of short-chain fatty acids ensures adequate absorption of fluids and electrolytes [in the colon](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/) and [promotes intestinal motility](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177).  
   
Some prebiotics are more easily digested than others. Some, such as inulin and fructooligosaccharides, may not be well tolerated in larger doses and can cause digestive [side effects](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/) such as gas, bloating, and diarrhea.  
   
These symptoms may be temporary as your digestive tract [adapts](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8970830/). To limit digestive upset, increase new fiber sources slowly and drink plenty of water to help move the fiber through your system.  
   
There are various prebiotic supplements available. Since different prebiotics affect different strains of good bacteria, it’s optimal to [take in a variety of prebiotics](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5622781/). A mixture of prebiotics may also most effectively support the beneficial actions of gut bacteria, like the production of [short-chain fatty acids](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8970830/).  
   
Omni-Biotic offers two different [prebiotic options](https://omnibioticlife.com/pages/product-overview) containing various beneficial fiber sources to meet your individual needs.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Constipation

![OMNi-BIOTiC Balance probiotic for immune support and gut comfort](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/balance-supplement.webp?v=1743544037)

Constipation, infrequent or difficult bowel movements, often of small, hard stools, is a [common problem](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/) affecting both adults and children.  
   
A lack of fiber and fluids can lead to constipation. Increasing fiber intake adds bulk and fluids to stool, making them softer and leading to easier passage and improved regularity.  
   
Insoluble fibers, such as those found in whole grains, vegetables, beans, nuts, and fruits, are most effective for [adding bulk to stool](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/). For fiber supplements, however, experts frequently recommend soluble fibers such as psyllium for constipation and [irritable bowel syndrome](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5548066/) due to their ability to hold fluid and lead to [softer stools](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177).  
   
Patients with constipation have been found in some studies to have lower levels of beneficial *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli*. [Several studies](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/) providing various species of *Bifidobacteria* probiotics found that they improved subjects’ constipation.  
   
Prebiotic inulin has also been found to relieve constipation in [some studies](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366) with adults and children. Additionally, [several synbiotics](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177) with various combinations of *Bifidobacteria*, *Lactobacilli,* and the prebiotic fibers FOS, GOS, and inulin have also been found to improve constipation.  
   
[Omni-Biotic Balance](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/balance/) is a synbiotic with multiple strains of beneficial bacteria selected to optimize digestion and reduce constipation. It includes species found to support the [protective gut barrier](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12602-017-9322-6#Tab2), enhancing its mucus production and improving [gut motility](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5421123/). It also provides small amounts of the prebiotics FOS and inulin to enhance its effects.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Diarrhea

![OMNi-BIOTiC AB 10 probiotic supplement for microbiome restoration and digestive balance](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/ab-10-probiotic.webp?v=1743544037)

Diarrhea can have many [causes](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/digestive-diseases/diarrhea/symptoms-causes), some acute such as infections, food poisoning, or traveler’s diarrhea, and some chronic, including food intolerances or chronic gastrointestinal conditions.  
   
Probiotics, particularly *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli* species, have been found to improve infectious [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/), possibly due to their ability to fight off gastrointestinal pathogens by maintaining the gut barrier and enhancing [immunity](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/).  
   
In addition, some probiotic strains have been associated with a reduced risk of antibiotic-associated diarrhea. Containing 10 species of *Lactobacilli* and *Bifidobacteria*, [Omni-Biotic AB 10](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/ab-10/) significantly reduced the risk of antibiotic-associated diarrhea in two separate studies in [surgical patients](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/BF03223333) and [nursing home residents](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7222499/).  
   
Some prebiotics can also help ward off diarrhea due to [GI pathogens](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366). [Research](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366) has found that supplements of the prebiotic fibers GOS and [inulin](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/) significantly reduced the risk of diarrhea in travelers to high-risk areas.  
   
Combining probiotics and beneficial prebiotics may be especially effective for combatting certain types of diarrhea. In [one study](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/), adding prebiotic inulin to a strain of *Bifidobacteria* improved the resolution of infectious diarrhea in children. [Research](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/) has also found that synbiotics prevented traveler’s diarrhea.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Optimized Digestion

[Gas and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/) are common symptoms of several common gastrointestinal conditions, including [irritable bowel syndrome (IBS)](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/ibs-and-probiotics/), small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO), constipation, and dietary intolerance. They can indicate an imbalance in the gut microbiome.

A [lack of regularity](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-and-poop/) resulting in constipation, diarrhea, or both, as can sometimes occur with IBS, can also indicate that your gut health is out of balance.

Probiotics and fiber can both play a part in reducing common gastrointestinal ailments and supporting a healthy gut.

### Probiotics for Digestion

Various strains of both *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli* have been associated with improved microbial balance and better digestive health, including better [regularity](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/) and a reduction in constipation, bloating, and other symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.  
   
A synbiotic composed of Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium, and FOS was found to reduce digestive symptoms of lactose intolerance in one small [study](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5622781/).

### Prebiotics for Digestion

[Research indicates](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8970830/) that the prebiotics inulin and FOS can improve bowel regularity and gut barrier function. In some studies, prebiotic GOS supplements [reduced some of the symptoms](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366) of patients with IBS.  
   
Research has found that acacia fiber, a soluble fiber also known as gum arabic, may have prebiotic properties and can increase beneficial *Lactobacilli* and *Bifidobacteria* and their production of short-chain fatty acids. One study of patients with irritable bowel syndrome found that yogurt with acacia fiber and a strain of *Bifidobacteria* [reduced their symptoms](http://www.mocedes.org/ajcer/volume9/AJCER-08-Daffaalla-2022.pdf).  
   
Omni-Biotic offers two prebiotic product options; [Omni-Logic Immune](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/omni-logic-immune/) provides acacia fiber and resistant starch, while [Omni-Logic Plus](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/omni-logic-plus/) provides FOS, GOS, and glucomannan.  
   
The non-digestible fibers in prebiotics can cause side effects in sensitive individuals, including those with irritable bowel syndrome or other gastrointestinal conditions. If you have a chronic digestive condition, consult your healthcare provider or registered dietitian before trying new fiber sources or probiotics.

## Key Takeaways

If you have ongoing bothersome gastrointestinal symptoms like diarrhea, gas, bloating, or constipation, it’s critical to seek health care from a qualified provider.   
Upping your fiber intake can benefit many common digestive conditions by supporting regularity and nourishing beneficial gut bacteria, in the case of prebiotics.  
   
Targeted use of probiotics can help balance your microbiome and lead to better digestive health, offering help for a variety of digestive ailments. Synbiotics can be a convenient way to enhance the health benefits of probiotics.

Omni-Biotic offers a variety of supplements to meet your digestive health needs. For a multi-strain probiotic formula proven to help prevent certain types of diarrhea, consider Omni-Biotic AB 10. Omni-Biotic Balance, on the other hand, can help optimize digestion in cases of constipation.  Consider Omni-Biotic’s prebiotic supplement line “Omni-Logic” for products with various beneficial fiber sources to optimize your gut health.

# Fiber and Probiotics: Better Together?

Updated on    by   Sara Chatfield

Share

* [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://omnibioticlife.com)
* [Twitter](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://omnibioticlife.com&text=)
* [Pinterest](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=//omnibioticlife.com/cdn/shopifycloud/storefront/assets/no-image-2048-a2addb12_1100x1100.gif&url=https://omnibioticlife.com&description=)
* [Copy Link](https://omnibioticlife.com)

![Close-up of fresh blueberries, a fiber-rich food that supports gut health alongside probiotics](//omnibioticlife.com/cdn/shop/articles/blueberries-fiber-gut-health.webp?v=1769124601&width=3840)

Probiotics and fiber are both critical for digestion and can improve common digestive ailments. Dietary fiber comes from plant foods and promotes digestive regularity. Some specialized types of fiber, called prebiotics, provide a food source for gut bacteria and may enhance the effects of probiotics.  
  
[Probiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/what-are-probiotics/) are beneficial bacteria found in dietary supplements and some foods. Fiber and probiotics can work together to support a healthy, balanced gut microbiome, which is critical for digestive and overall health.  
  
If you suffer from common digestive issues like [constipation](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/), [gas and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/), or [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/), you may have heard the advice to increase your fiber intake. Consuming adequate dietary fiber can improve your digestion and help with common gastrointestinal ailments.  
  
You may have also heard of using probiotics, live good bacteria, to improve gut health. But what about taking probiotics and fiber together? Certain types of fiber can work in tandem with probiotics to benefit digestive health, and this combination can be a key element of a probiotic and fiber diet.

## How Fiber and Probiotics Support One Another

Eating more fiber has been linked to improved digestive and overall health. High-fiber diets can prevent or relieve constipation and also reduce the risk of several chronic diseases. Unfortunately, many of our diets are too low in fiber due to a lack of whole plant foods and an over-reliance on animal products and highly processed foods.  
  
Estimates indicate that [95% of Americans](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6124841/) don’t meet the recommended dietary fiber intake of 19-38 grams daily. Western-style diets, including the typical U.S. diet, are also associated with lower diversity of beneficial gut microbes compared to [diets higher in fiber from plant foods](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33693453/).  
  
Dietary fiber is typically categorized into soluble and insoluble forms. Both are important for digestive health. Soluble fiber can dissolve in water to form a gel and helps to soften the stool. It also slows nutrient absorption and increases satiety. Insoluble fiber provides bulk for easier passage of stool. Most foods provide a mix of fiber types.

Common types of fiber include:

* **Insoluble sources:** Cellulose, hemicellulose, and lignin, found mainly in vegetables and whole grains.
* **Soluble sources:** Pectin, gums, and mucilages; pectins are found primarily in fruits and vegetables, whereas gums and mucilages are mainly extracted from plants for use in [processed foods](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5390821/).

[Prebiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/prebiotics-guide/) are specific types of soluble fiber that pass undigested through the upper gastrointestinal tract and go on to provide nourishment for the beneficial bacteria in the colon. They can work together with probiotics, a direct source of live beneficial bacteria. Prebiotics act as the food source for our gut bacteria, whereas probiotic supplements deliver live, beneficial bacteria to the gut to support balanced and diverse gut flora.

## What are the Benefits of Combining Fiber with Probiotics?

![Close-up of fiber-rich fruits and vegetables including pears, green apple, artichoke, and cruciferous vegetables](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/fruits-vegtables-for-health.webp?v=1743544037)

Both probiotics and dietary fiber can be beneficial for digestive health. But what happens when you combine them?

### The Role of Probiotics

Probiotics offer many benefits for digestive health, including enhanced digestion, protection against bad bacteria, and a reduction in digestive symptoms like [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/) and [constipation](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/).  
  
Although some foods, such as yogurt, kefir, sauerkraut, kimchi, and kombucha, are cultured with or have added probiotics, not all [probiotic foods](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/kombucha-vs-yogurt-vs-omnibiotic/) have enough live bacteria to survive the digestive system to provide health benefits. They may also not have the right combination of strains to have a lasting impact on your gut health.  
  
On the other hand, probiotic supplements typically provide information on the [CFU (colony-forming units)](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/what-are-colony-forming-units/) per gram of live active microorganisms, which ideally will be 107 to 1011.  
  
Finding a product with multiple strains of beneficial bacteria may be preferable since much of the research on common digestive issues, such as antibiotic-associated diarrhea and symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, has used [multi-strain products](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/types-of-probiotics/).

### The Role of Fiber

Adequate fiber intake is critical for digestive health; it keeps your gastrointestinal tract moving and your bowel movements regular and helps prevent chronic conditions.  
   
Increasing your intake of whole plant foods high in fiber, including whole grains, fruits, vegetables, legumes, nuts, and seeds, will provide a variety of insoluble and soluble dietary fibers, along with other crucial nutrients and beneficial plant compounds.  
   
Fiber supplements can be helpful if you’re looking to add a specific fiber source. Some varieties of fiber offer more benefits than others, depending on your health goals.

![Probiotic powder being mixed with water to prepare a gut health supplement](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/probiotics-powder.webp?v=1743544037)

### Combining Probiotics and Fiber

If you’re supplementing both probiotics and additional fiber, it’s typically [best to take them separately](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/can-you-take-prebiotics-and-probiotics-together/) since some of the beneficial bacteria can adhere to the fiber and be eliminated rather than [absorbed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7817516/).  
   
Prebiotic fibers work with the good bacteria in the digestive system by increasing their numbers and activity, including their ability to support immunity and maintain the gut barrier [against harmful pathogens](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Prebiotics can also enhance the benefits of probiotics.  
   
It’s preferable not to take probiotics and prebiotic fiber simultaneously because if the probiotics ferment the prebiotics too soon, this can cause [bothersome symptoms](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177) like [abdominal pain and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/).  
   
Synbiotics designed to be combined are the exception.   
[Synbiotics](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/can-you-take-prebiotics-and-probiotics-together/) are combination products that typically contain probiotics with a prebiotic, most commonly inulin, GOS, or FOS, that supports its beneficial [health effects](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7581511/). Research has found that synbiotics can enhance the microbiota, increasing beneficial *Bifidobacteria* and [*Lactobacilli*](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5622781/).  
   
The goal of a synbiotic is to maximize the health impact of both the good bacteria and the prebiotic fiber by providing them together. The amount of fiber in synbiotics varies, from very small quantities to larger amounts. Look for products that provide an optimal blend of probiotics with prebiotics.  
   
Omni-Biotics’ probiotic products are all synbiotics. Each Omni-Biotic probiotic contains prebiotic nutrients specifically selected for the probiotic strains in the formulation. These prebiotic fibers act as the food source for the specific probiotic bacteria in the Omni-Biotic powder. When Omni-Biotic probiotics are dissolved in water prior to intake, the probiotic bacteria rehydrate and consume these prebiotic fibers. This makes them strong for their passage through the GI tract, ensuring they arrive in the intestine alive and metabolically active.

## Is it Better to Take Prebiotics or Another Kind of Fiber?

Although all prebiotics are considered fiber, not all dietary fibers have prebiotic effects.   
The benefits of prebiotics for digestion go beyond those of standard fiber since they nourish beneficial bacteria, leading to a healthier, more balanced gut microbiome.  
   
Though humans have been consuming prebiotics in food since prehistoric times, research has uncovered their health benefits [much more recently](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Well-studied prebiotics include inulin, galactooligosaccharides (GOS), fructooligosaccharides (FOS), and resistant starch.  
   
[Prebiotics are found naturally](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/prebiotics-guide/) in some plant foods like chicory, artichokes, asparagus, onions, garlic, whole wheat, oats, and barley, as well as in some processed foods with added prebiotics. They are also available as supplements.   
Prebiotics target good bacteria in the colon, particularly *Bifidobacteria*. Research has found that consumption of the prebiotics [inulin, FOS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/), [resistant starch, and GOS](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4949558/) significantly increased levels of *Bifidobacteria* and other beneficial gut bacteria.  
   
The benefits to gut flora are specific to fiber with prebiotic effects. A recent analysis of randomized controlled trials found that prebiotic fiber supplements lead to significantly higher counts of *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli* than [non-prebiotic fiber supplements](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366).  
   
In addition to enhancing their diversity and activity, prebiotics can increase the gut bacteria’s production of short-chain fatty acids. These acids are critical in supporting the gut barrier and lowering colon pH. In turn, this promotes the growth of beneficial bacteria and [inhibits gut pathogens](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/). Enhanced production of short-chain fatty acids ensures adequate absorption of fluids and electrolytes [in the colon](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/) and [promotes intestinal motility](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177).  
   
Some prebiotics are more easily digested than others. Some, such as inulin and fructooligosaccharides, may not be well tolerated in larger doses and can cause digestive [side effects](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/) such as gas, bloating, and diarrhea.  
   
These symptoms may be temporary as your digestive tract [adapts](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8970830/). To limit digestive upset, increase new fiber sources slowly and drink plenty of water to help move the fiber through your system.  
   
There are various prebiotic supplements available. Since different prebiotics affect different strains of good bacteria, it’s optimal to [take in a variety of prebiotics](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5622781/). A mixture of prebiotics may also most effectively support the beneficial actions of gut bacteria, like the production of [short-chain fatty acids](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8970830/).  
   
Omni-Biotic offers two different [prebiotic options](https://omnibioticlife.com/pages/product-overview) containing various beneficial fiber sources to meet your individual needs.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Constipation

![OMNi-BIOTiC Balance probiotic for immune support and gut comfort](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/balance-supplement.webp?v=1743544037)

Constipation, infrequent or difficult bowel movements, often of small, hard stools, is a [common problem](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/do-probiotics-help-with-constipation/) affecting both adults and children.  
   
A lack of fiber and fluids can lead to constipation. Increasing fiber intake adds bulk and fluids to stool, making them softer and leading to easier passage and improved regularity.  
   
Insoluble fibers, such as those found in whole grains, vegetables, beans, nuts, and fruits, are most effective for [adding bulk to stool](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/). For fiber supplements, however, experts frequently recommend soluble fibers such as psyllium for constipation and [irritable bowel syndrome](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5548066/) due to their ability to hold fluid and lead to [softer stools](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177).  
   
Patients with constipation have been found in some studies to have lower levels of beneficial *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli*. [Several studies](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/) providing various species of *Bifidobacteria* probiotics found that they improved subjects’ constipation.  
   
Prebiotic inulin has also been found to relieve constipation in [some studies](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366) with adults and children. Additionally, [several synbiotics](https://www.europeanreview.org/article/16177) with various combinations of *Bifidobacteria*, *Lactobacilli,* and the prebiotic fibers FOS, GOS, and inulin have also been found to improve constipation.  
   
[Omni-Biotic Balance](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/balance/) is a synbiotic with multiple strains of beneficial bacteria selected to optimize digestion and reduce constipation. It includes species found to support the [protective gut barrier](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12602-017-9322-6#Tab2), enhancing its mucus production and improving [gut motility](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5421123/). It also provides small amounts of the prebiotics FOS and inulin to enhance its effects.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Diarrhea

![OMNi-BIOTiC AB 10 probiotic supplement for microbiome restoration and digestive balance](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0567/5057/1655/files/ab-10-probiotic.webp?v=1743544037)

Diarrhea can have many [causes](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/digestive-diseases/diarrhea/symptoms-causes), some acute such as infections, food poisoning, or traveler’s diarrhea, and some chronic, including food intolerances or chronic gastrointestinal conditions.  
   
Probiotics, particularly *Bifidobacteria* and *Lactobacilli* species, have been found to improve infectious [diarrhea](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-diarrhea/), possibly due to their ability to fight off gastrointestinal pathogens by maintaining the gut barrier and enhancing [immunity](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/).  
   
In addition, some probiotic strains have been associated with a reduced risk of antibiotic-associated diarrhea. Containing 10 species of *Lactobacilli* and *Bifidobacteria*, [Omni-Biotic AB 10](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/omnibiotic/ab-10/) significantly reduced the risk of antibiotic-associated diarrhea in two separate studies in [surgical patients](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/BF03223333) and [nursing home residents](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7222499/).  
   
Some prebiotics can also help ward off diarrhea due to [GI pathogens](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366). [Research](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/nbu.12366) has found that supplements of the prebiotic fibers GOS and [inulin](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3705355/) significantly reduced the risk of diarrhea in travelers to high-risk areas.  
   
Combining probiotics and beneficial prebiotics may be especially effective for combatting certain types of diarrhea. In [one study](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4908950/), adding prebiotic inulin to a strain of *Bifidobacteria* improved the resolution of infectious diarrhea in children. [Research](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4648921/) has also found that synbiotics prevented traveler’s diarrhea.

## Fiber vs. Probiotics for Optimized Digestion

[Gas and bloating](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-for-gas-and-bloating/) are common symptoms of several common gastrointestinal conditions, including [irritable bowel syndrome (IBS)](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/ibs-and-probiotics/), small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO), constipation, and dietary intolerance. They can indicate an imbalance in the gut microbiome.

A [lack of regularity](https://www.omnibioticlife.com/probiotics-and-poop/) resulting in constipation, diarrhea, or both, as can sometimes occur with IBS, can also indicate that your gut health is out of balance.

Probiotics and fiber can both play a part in reducing common gastrointestinal ailments and supporting a healthy gut.

## Gut Restoration

For those needing to restore their gut health following disruption from antimicrobial medication, infection, as well as years of stress on the body that may have manifested in GI issues and other health challenges.

##### Our gut microbiome needs balance and diversity in order to support healthy digestion and overall body function.

![](//omnibioticlife.com/cdn/shop/files/gut-restoration.svg?v=1711394517)

**Bifidobacteria (Good Gut Bacteria)**  
 Members of the genus Bifidobacterium were among the earliest microbes to colonize the human gastrointestinal tract, making them a foundational presence in the gut microbiome.

A Thriving Gut Microbiome

Research shows that a thriving gut microbiome with bacterial balance and diversity contributes to health, whereas a depleted and imbalanced gut microbiome is associated with many forms of disease. Signs of a stressed-out gut include diarrhea, bloating, skin flare-ups, low energy, weight gain, as well as headaches and joint pain.

Stressors on Your Gut

Everyday factors, such as stress, certain medications, travel and food-borne illness can deplete our gut microbiome. This creates an environment that is more prone to the overgrowth of pathogens and subsequent infection, such as from the dangerous C.diff bacteria. Think about it like a depleted ecosystem, where no life can flourish and there is room for “bottom feeders” and unwanted invaders to thrive.

Omni-Biotic to the Rescue

A very effective and seamless way to replenish the gut microbiome with beneficial bacteria which we need to live well and long is to regularly take a probiotic supplement.

Omni-Biotic AB 10 is designed to restore healthy bacterial diversity in the gut, and is clinically shown to reduce loose stool and risk of developing a secondary infection, like C. Diff.
